items.title: Design containment at the environment layer first
items.why: Sandbox and egress boundaries keep failures bounded even when the model, a tool, or a user prompt goes off the rails.
how_to_steps: Choose the isolation pattern that matches the user and task: ephemeral container, workspace sandbox, or sealed VM.
Default to network deny and limit writes to the workspace or explicitly mounted paths.
Keep credentials outside the sandbox and pass only scoped session tokens inside.
Delay project-local config parsing until after trust is established, and inspect tool output before it reaches model context.
items.evidence: Anthropic says its Claude Code sandbox cut permission prompts by 84%, auto mode catches about 83% of overeager behaviors, and Gray Swan prompt-injection success is roughly 0.1% on single attempts.
items.try_monday: Move one internal coding agent into a workspace-only sandbox with network denied by default and audit what access it still truly needs.

items.title: Put a gateway in front of providers so outages do not stop the app
items.why: Routing through a gateway gives you model failover, provider order, and request-level visibility instead of a single brittle dependency.
how_to_steps: Route traffic through one gateway endpoint instead of wiring the app directly to a single provider SDK.
Set a primary model and a fallback models list in providerOptions.gateway.
Add provider ordering and fast timeouts so the gateway fails over quickly when a provider stalls.
Inspect modelAttempts and providerAttempts metadata, then monitor latency, spend, and failure patterns by route.
items.evidence: Vercel says AI Gateway is the same system that has powered v0 for millions of users, sits on infrastructure delivering sub-20ms latency, and removes the single-provider failure mode.
items.try_monday: Wrap one production AI endpoint with a primary model plus two fallbacks and log which model actually serves each request.
